      "text": "Memory schema: facts are atomic with no repetition; fact types are 'world' and 'experience'; tags serve as primary categorization; provenance tracked by document_id and chunk_id; timestamps include mentioned_at for conflict resolution and occurred_start/end for event timeline; deduplication by text hash or document_id+chunk_id; priority tiers: directives > canonical > tags > experiences > behavioral rules.",

    {
      "id": "9f9d3d23-8526-4a7c-9374-e0702044444d",
      "text": "Memory system uses Hindsight MCP self-hosted on OCI VM (ARM64, 2 OCPU, 12 GB RAM, Oracle Linux 9) with a single bank root named hermes (canonical v1) and 9 banks: hermes, toolset (445 facts), researchit (124), kairos (68), cl-concerts-db (45), evidencia-zero (30), yacv (29), witral (11), default (56). Group banks are created dynamically via /onboarding. Session lifecycle: each session starts with recall(hermes) to load context and ends with retain(hermes) to persist facts. Retrieval prioritizes observations, falls back to raw recall if stale.       "text": "Memory schema uses fact types (world+experience), tags, provenance (document_id+chunk_id), and timestamps (mentioned_at for conflict resolution, occurred_start/occurred_end for timeline).     {
      "id": "ac35406f-5c9c-44cf-b52b-4bc778e7e85c",
      "text": "Three documented gaps: no memory pruning/archiving policy, no freshness decay for aged facts, no cross-fact-bank consolidation strategy. | These are known areas for future improvement.",

      "date": "2026-06-29T05:28:43.513285+00:00",

    {
      "id": "ad9123e5-ea2d-4de4-920b-5381b4250857",
      "text": "Heuristics include atomic facts with no repetition, temporal anchoring with mentioned_at and occurred_start/end, tag-first retrieval via recall, persona directives for behavioral steering, and provenance tracking through document_id chains. | Describes the active heuristics of the memory system.",
      "context": "heuristics-synthesis",

      "text": "Session lifecycle: each session starts with recall(hermes) to load context and ends with retain(hermes) to persist facts. WhatsApp group context is loaded from channel_aliases.json, updated every 10 minutes by a cron job.",

      "text": "The memory system uses Hindsight MCP with multiple banks (hermes, researchit, kairos, cl-concerts-db, evidencia-zero, yacv, witral, default) and dynamic group banks.       "text": "On June 29, 2026, a routing failure occurred because the LLM made routing decisions instead of programmatic code, violating the deterministic YAML-based routing design.       "id": "dc8009a2-5bde-476a-8be3-0856480f8122",
      "text": "Memory schema: facts are atomic with no repetition; fact types are 'world' and 'experience'; tags serve as primary categorization; provenance tracked by document_id and chunk_id; timestamps include mentioned_at for conflict resolution and occurred_start/end for event timeline; deduplication by text hash or document_id+chunk_id; priority tiers: directives > canonical > tags > experiences > behavioral rules.",

    {
      "id": "9f9d3d23-8526-4a7c-9374-e0702044444d",
      "text": "Memory system uses Hindsight MCP self-hosted on OCI VM (ARM64, 2 OCPU, 12 GB RAM, Oracle Linux 9) with a single bank root named hermes (canonical v1) and 9 banks: hermes, toolset (445 facts), researchit (124), kairos (68), cl-concerts-db (45), evidencia-zero (30), yacv (29), witral (11), default (56). Group banks are created dynamically via /onboarding. Session lifecycle: each session starts with recall(hermes) to load context and ends with retain(hermes) to persist facts. Retrieval prioritizes observations, falls back to raw recall if stale. Temporal reasoning: conflict resolution uses the latest mentioned_at; timeline reconstruction orders by occurred_start/end, not mentioned_at; no averaging, summing, or favoring based on position or explicit date.",

      "id": "f12c3506-f14c-4717-8976-6d9b66fac2a6",
      "text": "Retrieval strategy attempts search_observations() first, falls back to recall(), then expand(). Observations have a stale flag. Tag-first retrieval uses semantic context tags as the primary axis.",

      "date": "2026-06-29T05:28:43.513285+00:00",

      "id": "592f31fc-17db-43c2-8503-8701cb042b39",
      "text": "Memory schema uses fact types (world+experience), tags, provenance (document_id+chunk_id), and timestamps (mentioned_at for conflict resolution, occurred_start/occurred_end for timeline). Priority tiers: directives > canonical > tags > experiences > behavioral rules. Facts are atomic and deduped by text hash or document_id+chunk_id. | To ensure memory consistency and efficient retrieval",

    {
      "id": "ac35406f-5c9c-44cf-b52b-4bc778e7e85c",
      "text": "Three documented gaps: no memory pruning/archiving policy, no freshness decay for aged facts, no cross-fact-bank consolidation strategy. | These are known areas for future improvement.",

      "date": "2026-06-29T05:28:43.513285+00:00",

    {
      "id": "ad9123e5-ea2d-4de4-920b-5381b4250857",
      "text": "Heuristics include atomic facts with no repetition, temporal anchoring with mentioned_at and occurred_start/end, tag-first retrieval via recall, persona directives for behavioral steering, and provenance tracking through document_id chains. | Describes the active heuristics of the memory system.",
      "context": "heuristics-synthesis",
      "date": "2026-06-29T05:28:43.513285+00:00",
      "fact_type": "world",
      "document_id": "d4f48a65-5fc2-4f2f-95ed-fee54ad76d7c",

    {
      "id": "f378e6e1-da5b-4f69-8a06-8c88c72e74fc",
      "text": "Session lifecycle: each session starts with recall(hermes) to load context and ends with retain(hermes) to persist facts. WhatsApp group context is loaded from channel_aliases.json, updated every 10 minutes by a cron job.",
      "context": "heuristics-synthesis",
      "date": "2026-06-29T05:28:43.513285+00:00",
      "fact_type": "world",

    {
      "id": "bc1977ba-b925-4012-99b4-be0dcc8ce29a",
      "text": "Retention rules prefer factual/behavioral over ephemeral, deduplicate by text hash or document_id+chunk_id, inject context into recall queries.",

    {
      "id": "6dc5ddca-3f6c-41e4-9488-1727743e696d",
      "text": "Retention prefers factual/behavioral facts over ephemeral, deduplicates, and injects context into queries. | System heuristic about retention behavior.",

    {
      "id": "759d3dad-849f-4c3c-b108-fc9dd3c15e0d",
      "text": "Retrieval strategy uses search_observations() first, with fallback to recall() and then expand(). Observations have stale flag. Tag-first retrieval with semantic context tags as primary axis.",

      "text": "Routing is YAML-based deterministic across 6 groups. Kanban returns messages to originating_group. Root cause of routing failures: LLM decision vs programmatic routing.",

      "text": "Behavioral rules are stored in SOUL.md, permanent unless overridden, governing assistant's dialect and style: Chilean inclination, no rioplatense voseo, accepts idioms (al tiro, caleta, we\u00f3n), concise, no emojis, no corporate language, no filler phrases, no em dashes, no forced positivity. | Involving: user",

    {

      "text": "User prefers deepseek-v4-flash for text, gpt-4o for vision, and Groq Whisper whisper-large-v3-turbo for speech-to-text; prefers concise Spanish neutral with Chilean inclination, no emojis/corporate/filler; user is Chilean and detests Argentine dialect, so neutral Spanish with Chilean inclination is used, accepting idioms like 'al tiro', 'caleta', 'we\u00f3n'. | Involving: user",
